Empire State Pride Agenda records, 1990-2013.
Collection Number: 7630

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Empire State Pride Agenda records, 1990-2013.
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
7630
Abstract:
Subject files, publicity items, programs for fund raising and political events, news and flyers on gay and lesbian issues; also, minutes (1991-1994), chronological files on Pride Agenda's activities, posters, t-shirts, stickers, and other ephemera relating to fund raising and political events.
Creator:
Empire State Pride Agenda
Dadey, Richard D.
Piel, Candida Scott.
Post, Libby.
Parrish, Lisa.
D'Alessio, Mark.
FAIRPAC
New York State Gay and Lesbian Lobby
Quanitities:
75.2 cubic feet.
5 mapcase folders.
Language:
Collection material in English
ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY

Incorporated in 1990 as the result of a merger between two statewide organizations: the New York State Lesbian and Gay Lobby based in Albany, and FAIRPAC based in New York City. The Empire State Pride Agenda is a political organization that lobbies the New York State government for legal protection and equal rights for lesbians, gay men, and bisexuals. Libby Post and Mark D'Alessio were the Pride Agenda's first co-chairs of the Board, and Richard D. Dadey, Jr., the first executive director (following Lisa Parrish as acting executive director for three months). The new organization was more successful at fundraising.
Maintaining offices both in Albany and New York City, the first issue the Pride Agenda tackled was the creation of a New York City council district drawn in a way that included enough gay and lesbian supportive voters to make the election of a lesbian or gay city councilperson a possibility. This was achieved in 1991.
The Pride Agenda adopts legislative agendas each year and has lobbied on the state level for a bias-related violence bill, a civil rights bill, and a domestic partnership bill. The organization endorses pro-gay candidates for local and state office, and remains active in New York City politics. In July 1998, New York City mayor Rudolph Giuliani signed into law legislation recognizing domestic partners in New York City.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION

Subject files, publicity items, programs for fund raising and political events, news and flyers on gay and lesbian issues; also, minutes (1991-1994), chronological files on Pride Agenda's activities, posters, t-shirts, stickers, and other ephemera relating to fund raising and political events. Also includes large numbers of audio visual materials such as DVDs, CD, and videos as well as the files of Joe Tarver consisting of photographs, news clippings and event files.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

Empire State Pride Agenda Records, #7630. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Separated Materials

Publications removed from the collection and cataloged individually in the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections.
Separated Publications
  1. Pitman, Gayle. This Day in June, 2014
  2. Wahls, Zach. My Two Moms: Lessons of Love, Strength, and What Makes a Family, 2012
SUBJECTS

Subjects:
Unmarried couples -- Legal status, laws, etc.
Civil rights.
Bias (Law).
Lesbians -- Legal status, laws, etc.
Gays -- Legal status, laws, etc.
Homosexuality -- Law and legislation.

Box 73 Folder 3
New York AIDS Coalition Information
 2004-05
Box 41 Folder 3
Civil Rights
 1997-1998
Scope and Contents
Contains correspondence from Senator Joseph Bruno, other correspondence between ESPA, a memo regarding 'Antidiscrimination Protections for Gay Men and Lesbians in New York State', transcript of the January 8, 1997 regular session of the Senate, news articles and informational packets
Box 41 Folder 19
Miscellaneous - Hate Crimes and Marriage Equality
 1999-06; 1999-09; 2004-03-03; 2005-04-06
Scope and Contents
Includes ESPA Press Advisories/Press Releases, 'News from Attorney General Eliot Spitzer', and a letter from Anthony Crowell regarding the NYC support of marriage equality following a mayoral announcement.
Box 41 Folder 20
SONDA Information Packet
 1997
Scope and Contents
Includes a Prefiled copy of the bill, a memo regarding Antidiscrimination Protections for Lesbians and Gay Men in New York State, and general information on SONDA

Campaign Materials, 1998-2005
Box 50 Folder 1
1996 Election Information
 1996
Scope and Contents
Includes the Empire State Pride Agenda's New York State Voter Guide for November 5., 1996 (x2); a letter from the Ulster County Democrat Committee endorsing Maurice Hinchey; and a mailer from the Lambda Democrats of Bronx County.
Box 50 Folder 2
Fall 1998 Campaign Logistics and Information
 1998
Scope and Contents
Contains a copy of "1998 Facts for Voters" by The League of Women Voters of New York State; a Lambda Democrats of Bronx County Voters' Guide/Palm Card; assorted Empire State Pride Agenda requests for Volunteers for the Fall 1998 cycle; various campaign materials;

Box 16 Folder 46
Jeanne Newland
 May-June 2003
Scope and Contents
She had been denied New York jobless benefits usually given to same sex couples when following a partner taking a job elsewhere. Governor Pataki stepped in and allowed the benefits.
